column1: #   digest = 16 lowercase hex chars; immutable, committed). The loader
#   (corpus::load_checked) re-derives this and HARD-ERRORS on any drift, an
#   unknown shape_class, a malformed row, or a missing snapshot — by design.
# Re-fetching a URL means a NEW row + NEW snapshot, never editing an old one.
# shape_class ∈ {wikipedia, sec_edgar, regulator, news, tech_blog, hub_index,
# PROVENANCE: every row below was fetched from the LIVE public web on the
# title + body content; no SEC rate-limit / Cloudflare / login / error /
# redirect-stub pages). The earlier 3 synthetic hand-crafted fixtures have
# been REMOVED (rows and snapshot files); this corpus is now ALL-REAL.
# REPRODUCIBILITY DISCLOSURE (honest scope of the immutable-corpus contract):
# the contract is "these exact committed snapshot bytes, forever" — the loader
# (corpus::load_checked) hard-errors on any filename/URL drift or missing
# snapshot, so that contract DOES hold for every row here. It is NOT a claim
# that each capture can be re-fetched and re-validated against its live source.
# A substantial subset (~15+ rows) is intrinsically non-reproducible: the news
# sections (8), the hub_index landing/aggregator pages (5), and the
# daily/continuously-updated regulator pages (e.g. Federal Reserve H.15
# Selected Interest Rates, dated "Daily"; Bank of England Bank Rate; the
# gov.uk/Fed hubs) all change at the source between fetches. For those rows the
# committed bytes are a frozen point-in-time capture and the only authority is
# the file in this repo — they are deliberately NOT presented as stable,
# re-checkable-against-live captures. Stable, re-fetchable rows (dated SEC
# EDGAR filings by accession, dated blog posts, the dated W3C HTML 5.2
# Recommendation, IANA/example.* reserved domains) are the re-checkable subset.
# EDGAR / table-heavy coverage (the weak point this harness exists to catch):
# the deliberate EDGAR-exclusion caveat that previously stood here is now
# FALSE and has been removed. This corpus DOES include real table-heavy SEC
# EDGAR 10-K filings and real table-heavy regulator pages:
#     <table>, full 10-K prose incl. "Part I Item 1. Business").
#     table-heavy EDGAR HTML — the classic EDGAR weak point).
#   * sec_edgar, Berkshire FY2024 & Microsoft FY2025 10-Ks, Apple Q2-FY2026
#     (22 <table>), gov.uk income-tax rates (7 <table>), US Federal Reserve
#     open-market ops (16 <table>) and H.15 selected interest rates.
# Stages 6-8 passing on this file IS therefore evidence about EDGAR/table
# extraction and regulator filings. (HLD §11 item 4 corpus-curation work;
# 51 real URLs. Per shape_class: wikipedia 8, sec_edgar 8, regulator 8,
